title: malus japonica
intro: Malus japonica, also known as the Japanese crabapple, is a species of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to various health benefits. Malus japonica is valued for its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestive health, and aid in weight loss due to its high fiber and pectin content. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into jams and other food products.
